name: Onion Soup (Emeril)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add bacon and cook until fat renders and the bacon is crispy, about 5-7 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels; set aside for garnish.
3. In the same pan, add yellow, red, and white onions, shallots, and leeks. Cook, stirring occasionally, until they begin to wilt, about 10 minutes.
4. Add scallions (white parts only) and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
5. Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir well to coat. Cook, stirring frequently, until the mixture turns golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
6. Gradually add chicken stock, whisking constantly to avoid lumps. Bring to a simmer and then reduce heat to low, continuing to cook for 30 minutes.
7. Stir in cream, salt, and pepper to taste. Mix well.
8. Using an immersion blender or food processor, puree about one-third of the soup (including solids) until smooth. Return this puréed portion to the saucepan.
9. Serve hot, garnished with crispy bacon pieces and snipped chives.
Yield: 4 servings
